scores.correctness_notes: Captures CertificateAvailableEvent correctly but entirely omits CertificateDeniedEvent. Misses key trigger conditions from gold standard (revocation, request_errors with codes 101/102/103/201). Describes interface contract details differently (no mention of CSR requirements, requirer/provider databag obligations). Core events and when-to-request scenarios are only partially covered.
scores.hallucination_notes: The response describes TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, CertificateRequestAttributes, renewal_relative_time, renew_certificate(), regenerate_private_key(), CertificatesRequirerCharmEvents — these are real APIs from the charms.tls_certificates v4 library (a different library than the gold standard's charmlibs-interfaces-tls-certificates v1). Not pure fabrication, but applied to the wrong library context, causing minor inaccuracies.
